text: I saw him near the docks on Tuesday. He was with a man called Reeves, or so I thought. I would rather not say more.
The docks were empty that night. I heard footsteps on the cobbles. Someone shouted Reeves' name. Then silence.
Margaret Hayes is mistaken. I was at the warehouse until midnight. Reeves was not there. I am certain of it. Ask Whitfield; he was drinking.
Nothing unusual at the warehouse that week.
The accountant left the office at six. He carried a leather satchel. Someone followed him down River Lane.
I saw the numbers guy near the station. He looked nervous. That is all I know.
I have worked the docks for twenty years. I saw two figures near pier seven. They argued. I stayed quiet. Men like that do not like witnesses.
A black motorcar stopped outside my shop. Two men got out. One wore a grey overcoat. I have told you people already.
The grey overcoat man came back on Thursday. He asked for directions to the docks. I gave them. Nothing more to add.
The motorcar had no plates. Grey overcoat again. I am not repeating myself for a third time.
I was home by eight. My wife can confirm it. I have no knowledge of the incident.
You people keep asking the same questions. I already gave my statement. Leave me alone.
The lamp by the quay was out. I noticed because I walk that route every evening. I saw no one unusual.
I do not appreciate being dragged in again. Your detectives wasted my time last week. I have nothing new to say.
